diff --git a/CEO_NOTES.md b/CEO_NOTES.md
index b1bebb3..8d9f357 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -165,2 +165,19 @@
   - Ads scaled to S2 content $12,000/day.
 - Watch next week: A $89 conversion, cash rebuild after R&D, tier 4 headroom, support backlog age, churn, and compute cost under larger quotas.
+
+## Week 11 / Day 70
+
+- Cash: $463,930. Subscribers: 17,794. Week 10 added 6,567 subscribers from 11,798 leads; cash fell after the prior R&D spend but operating cash flow is now strong.
+- New S2 conversion at A $89 remained healthy: content S2 produced 10,713 leads from $84,000 and about 5,985 paid content subscribers. Subscription payments were $565,239.
+- Costs last week: ops -$147,000, ads -$84,000, compute -$32,800, capacity -$28,000, dev -$9,100, lead acquisition -$5,929. The core S2 engine is cash-positive even with heavier support.
+- Active mix: S2 A 17,596, S2 B 126; S1/S3 remain small. A remains the volume product and B/C adoption is still marginal.
+- Issues: 1,471 open on the dashboard. Most S2 issues are fresh, but S1 still has very old unresolved issues and a few S2 issues are aging; support reputation is now the biggest near-term churn risk.
+- Social continues to show quota and ticket-latency complaints despite high quality. The product is valued, but heavy users hit ceilings and support queues.
+- Changes:
+  - New prices: A $99, B $249, C $449. S2 first-month promo remains $10, so new S2 A cash is $89.
+  - Quotas raised to A 10000, B 40000, C 100000 to reduce hard-cap complaints.
+  - Ops increased to $30,500/day total: global $8,000, S1 $4,000, S2 $18,000, S3 $500.
+  - Dev increased modestly to $1,900/day total: global $200, S1 $100, S2 $1,500, S3 $100.
+  - Ads scaled to S2 content $18,000/day only.
+  - No social post; prior operational posts have been unreliable, and execution matters more.
+- Watch next week: A $99 conversion, support backlog age, churn after older low-price cohorts renew, tier 4 capacity headroom, and compute cost after another quota increase.
